title: We need to take over an existing app | Bc. Ondřej Halata (halatao.cz)

seo.title: We need to take over an existing app | Bc. Ondřej Halata (halatao.cz)
seo.description: I help companies take over existing or partially delivered web applications, map delivery risk, and define the next steps without forcing a rewrite first.

page.title: We need to take over an existing app | Bc. Ondřej Halata (halatao.cz)
page.h1: We need to take over an existing app safely
page.description: I help companies take over existing or partially delivered web applications, map delivery risk, and define the next steps without forcing a rewrite first.
page.primaryQuery: we need to take over an existing app

hero.title: The app is important, but the next change feels risky
hero.subtitle: A common situation after a supplier change, a team transition, or a period without strong technical ownership.

intro: Taking over an inherited application is a sensitive moment. The company needs progress, but it also needs to avoid breaking operations or investing in the wrong technical direction.
The real risk is usually in hidden dependencies, unclear release flow, and knowledge that remained with the previous supplier or team.
That is why the first step should be structured discovery, not dramatic rebuilding.

sections.title: Typical symptoms
body: Without an initial map of the system, teams often fix visible symptoms while the underlying operational and delivery risks remain untouched.
bullets: fear of touching working parts of the system

sections.title: How I approach it
body: I start with codebase orientation, dependency review, environment checks, and the most critical business workflows. From there we can set priorities grounded in impact instead of guesswork.

sections.title: What a good outcome looks like
body: The goal is not a quick patch. The goal is to restore control, clarity, and a safer path for future delivery.
bullets: faster onboarding into the inherited stack
less pressure to rewrite by default

faq.question: Do you need full access from day one?
faq.answer: Ideally yes, but many handovers happen in stages. Source code, infrastructure, release flow, and business context are the most important starting points.

faq.question: How quickly can we tell whether larger changes are needed?
faq.answer: A focused review usually shows the main risks quickly. Bigger refactors or rewrites should come only after that picture is clear.

faq.question: Can takeover work happen while the app stays live?
faq.answer: Yes. That is the normal case. It is one reason I prefer staged stabilisation over abrupt rebuild decisions.

faq.question: Can you stay involved after the review?
faq.answer: Yes. I can continue with delivery or hand over a structured plan to your internal team.

for: companies after a supplier or team change
buyers who need control before committing to bigger change
notFor: simple content sites with no application logic
situations with no access to code or environments

cta.label: Explore the project fit
cta.note: Share the current state, the main risk, and what needs to change next.
